summary: An excellent sound‑board capture of the legendary 3‑29‑90 Nassau show, highlighted by a monster Dark Star, soaring Bird Song and an unforgettable Knockin' on Heaven's Door.
review: The recording is praised as crystal‑clear, 5‑star soundboard quality that lets every nuance shine. Musically the concert is lauded for its fluid second set with Branford Marsalis, delivering a towering Dark Star, a 16‑minute Eyes of the World and one of the best Knockin' on Heaven’s Door performances ever. Overall the show is described as emotionally charged, improvisationally rich, and a must‑have for any Dead collector.

ai_rating.rationale: Over twenty enthusiastic reviews rate the performance 4–5 stars and repeatedly call the sound quality 5/5; minor first‑set criticisms do not outweigh the overwhelming praise.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers give the audio a perfect 5/5, describing it as crisp, clear and superior to other copies; no significant drop‑outs or mix problems are noted.

standout_songs: Throwing Stones > Turn On Your Lovelight
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers repeatedly note a fluid, flowing performance that never feels rushed; the second set especially shines with Branford Marsalis adding a jazzy edge and several extended jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for emotional vocal delivery, soaring guitar work and ‘fighting to save’ the Promised Land; described as climbing strings fluidly.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for an awesome 15‑minute Estimated Prophet and strong rhythm work throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as powerful, especially during the Drum segment leading into Space; overall drumming praised for intensity.
